bnpl: Buy Now Pay Later - Core product allowing consumers to split purchases into interest-free installments. Consumers pay 1/4 upfront and the remaining in 3 installments over 6 weeks.
pi4: Pay-in-4 - Flagship BNPL product. Consumers pay 25% upfront and split remaining balance into 3 equal installments paid every 2 weeks.
order: A purchase transaction made by a consumer at a merchant. Primary unit of analysis.
order_id: Unique identifier for an order. Primary key for order-level analysis.
consumer: A customer who uses the platform to make purchases. Identified by consumer_id or consumer_uuid.
consumer_id: Unique numeric identifier for a consumer. Primary key for consumer-level analysis.
merchant: A business that accepts the platform as a payment method. Identified by merchant_id.
merchant_id: Unique identifier for a merchant. Primary key for merchant-level analysis.
installment: One of the 4 scheduled payments a consumer must make for an order.
gpv: Gross Payment Volume - Total dollar value of orders processed. Key metric for business size.
gmv: Gross Merchandise Value - Same as GPV. Total value of goods sold through the platform.
merchant_revenue: Revenue earned from merchants, typically a percentage of order amount (merchant fee). Primary revenue source.
merchant_fee: Percentage of order amount that merchant pays. Varies by merchant, country, and channel.
ntl: Net Transaction Loss - Key risk metric. Measures actual loss per order including overdue, chargebacks, late fees, and fraud.
overdue: Amount of installment payments not paid by consumer after due date. Component of NTL.
chargeback: Disputed transaction reversed by card network. Consumer disputes charge with their bank.
gross_profit: Revenue minus direct costs. Key profitability metric before risk losses.
variable_profit: Gross profit minus variable costs including risk losses. Primary profitability metric.
nac: New Active Customer - Number of consumers who successfully make their first order. Key acquisition metric.
channel: Where order originates: online (merchant website), instore (physical retail), mobile (app), portal (website).
online: Orders placed through merchant websites. Largest channel.
instore: Orders placed at physical retail locations. Uses card or barcode.
settlement: When merchant ships order and the platform pays merchant. Triggers merchant revenue charge.
late_fee: Fee charged to consumer when they miss an installment payment.
aov: Average Order Value - Total GPV divided by total number of unique orders.
consumer_country_code: Country code for consumer. Common values: AU, US, GB, NZ, CA.
sales_channel: Field indicating where order was placed: Online, Instore.
